preview_text:
The model favors Switzerland with an 81% win probability, leaving Qatar just 3% to claim victory. That gap reflects a substantial asymmetry in expected quality. Qatar's draw chance stands at 16%, slightly above its win probability—a plausible outcome but not the likeliest scenario.

The spread across outcomes shows Switzerland as clear favorite but not dominant in the extreme. A draw occurs in roughly one of every six simulations, suggesting the match carries genuine competitive possibility rather than being a formality. Qatar's path to victory remains narrow, though not impossible at a neutral venue.

The model and sportsbooks are closely aligned. Switzerland's win probability differs by less than 3 percentage points between the two sources, and draws sit within 1 point. Polymarket prices track similarly, offering no material divergence. The consensus view across all three sources treats this as a decisive Swiss advantage with modest draw risk.
